root_text.an5.57:1.1: “Pañcimāni, bhikkhave, ṭhānāni abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbāni itthiyā vā purisena vā gahaṭṭhena vā pabbajitena vā.
root_text.an5.57:1.3: ‘Jarādhammomhi, jaraṁ anatīto’ti abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ itthiyā vā purisena vā gahaṭṭhena vā pabbajitena vā.
root_text.an5.57:1.4: ‘Byādhidhammomhi, byādhiṁ anatīto’ti abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ itthiyā vā purisena vā gahaṭṭhena vā pabbajitena vā.
root_text.an5.57:1.5: ‘Maraṇadhammomhi, maraṇaṁ anatīto’ti abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ itthiyā vā purisena vā gahaṭṭhena vā pabbajitena vā.
root_text.an5.57:1.6: ‘Sabbehi me piyehi manāpehi nānābhāvo vinābhāvo’ti abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ itthiyā vā purisena vā gahaṭṭhena vā pabbajitena vā.
root_text.an5.57:1.8: Yaṁ kammaṁ karissāmi—kalyāṇaṁ vā pāpakaṁ vā—
root_text.an5.57:1.9: tassa dāyādo bhavissāmī’ti abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ itthiyā vā purisena vā gahaṭṭhena vā pabbajitena vā.
root_text.an5.57:2.1: Kiñca, bhikkhave, atthavasaṁ paṭicca ‘jarādhammomhi, jaraṁ anatīto’ti abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ itthiyā vā purisena vā gahaṭṭhena vā pabbajitena vā?
root_text.an5.57:2.2: Atthi, bhikkhave, sattānaṁ yobbane yobbanamado, yena madena mattā kāyena duccaritaṁ caranti, vācāya duccaritaṁ caranti, manasā duccaritaṁ caranti.
root_text.an5.57:2.3: Tassa taṁ ṭhānaṁ abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhato yo yobbane yobbanamado so sabbaso vā pahīyati tanu vā pana hoti.
root_text.an5.57:2.4: Idaṁ kho, bhikkhave, atthavasaṁ paṭicca ‘jarādhammomhi, jaraṁ anatīto’ti abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ itthiyā vā purisena vā gahaṭṭhena vā pabbajitena vā.
root_text.an5.57:3.1: Kiñca, bhikkhave, atthavasaṁ paṭicca ‘byādhidhammomhi, byādhiṁ anatīto’ti abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ itthiyā vā purisena vā gahaṭṭhena vā pabbajitena vā?
root_text.an5.57:3.2: Atthi, bhikkhave, sattānaṁ ārogye ārogyamado, yena madena mattā kāyena duccaritaṁ caranti, vācāya duccaritaṁ caranti, manasā duccaritaṁ caranti.
root_text.an5.57:3.3: Tassa taṁ ṭhānaṁ abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhato yo ārogye ārogyamado so sabbaso vā pahīyati tanu vā pana hoti.
root_text.an5.57:3.4: Idaṁ kho, bhikkhave, atthavasaṁ paṭicca ‘byādhidhammomhi, byādhiṁ anatīto’ti abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ itthiyā vā purisena vā gahaṭṭhena vā pabbajitena vā.
root_text.an5.57:4.1: Kiñca, bhikkhave, atthavasaṁ paṭicca ‘maraṇadhammomhi, maraṇaṁ anatīto’ti abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ itthiyā vā purisena vā gahaṭṭhena vā pabbajitena vā?
root_text.an5.57:4.2: Atthi, bhikkhave, sattānaṁ jīvite jīvitamado, yena madena mattā kāyena duccaritaṁ caranti, vācāya duccaritaṁ caranti, manasā duccaritaṁ caranti.
root_text.an5.57:4.3: Tassa taṁ ṭhānaṁ abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhato yo jīvite jīvitamado so sabbaso vā pahīyati tanu vā pana hoti.
root_text.an5.57:4.4: Idaṁ kho, bhikkhave, atthavasaṁ paṭicca ‘maraṇadhammomhi, maraṇaṁ anatīto’ti abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ itthiyā vā purisena vā gahaṭṭhena vā pabbajitena vā.
root_text.an5.57:5.1: Kiñca, bhikkhave, atthavasaṁ paṭicca ‘sabbehi me piyehi manāpehi nānābhāvo vinābhāvo’ti abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ itthiyā vā purisena vā gahaṭṭhena vā pabbajitena vā?
root_text.an5.57:5.2: Atthi, bhikkhave, sattānaṁ piyesu manāpesu yo chandarāgo yena rāgena rattā kāyena duccaritaṁ caranti, vācāya duccaritaṁ caranti, manasā duccaritaṁ caranti.
root_text.an5.57:5.3: Tassa taṁ ṭhānaṁ abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhato yo piyesu manāpesu chandarāgo so sabbaso vā pahīyati tanu vā pana hoti.
root_text.an5.57:5.4: Idaṁ kho, bhikkhave, atthavasaṁ paṭicca ‘sabbehi me piyehi manāpehi nānābhāvo vinābhāvo’ti abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ itthiyā vā purisena vā gahaṭṭhena vā pabbajitena vā.
root_text.an5.57:6.1: Kiñca, bhikkhave, atthavasaṁ paṭicca ‘kammassakomhi, kammadāyādo kammayoni kammabandhu kammapaṭisaraṇo, yaṁ kammaṁ karissāmi—
root_text.an5.57:6.2: kalyāṇaṁ vā pāpakaṁ vā—tassa dāyādo bhavissāmī’ti abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ itthiyā vā purisena vā gahaṭṭhena vā pabbajitena vā?
root_text.an5.57:6.3: Atthi, bhikkhave, sattānaṁ kāyaduccaritaṁ vacīduccaritaṁ manoduccaritaṁ.
root_text.an5.57:6.4: Tassa taṁ ṭhānaṁ abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhato sabbaso vā duccaritaṁ pahīyati tanu vā pana hoti.
root_text.an5.57:6.5: Idaṁ kho, bhikkhave, atthavasaṁ paṭicca ‘kammassakomhi, kammadāyādo kammayoni kammabandhu kammapaṭisaraṇo, yaṁ kammaṁ karissāmi—
root_text.an5.57:6.7: tassa dāyādo bhavissāmī’ti abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ itthiyā vā purisena vā gahaṭṭhena vā pabbajitena vā.
root_text.an5.57:7.1: Sa kho so, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako iti paṭisañcikkhati:
root_text.an5.57:7.2: ‘na kho ahaññeveko jarādhammo jaraṁ anatīto, atha kho yāvatā sattānaṁ āgati gati cuti upapatti sabbe sattā jarādhammā jaraṁ anatītā’ti.
root_text.an5.57:7.3: Tassa taṁ ṭhānaṁ abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhato maggo sañjāyati.
root_text.an5.57:7.4: So taṁ maggaṁ āsevati bhāveti bahulīkaroti.
root_text.an5.57:7.5: Tassa taṁ maggaṁ āsevato bhāvayato bahulīkaroto saṁyojanāni sabbaso pahīyanti anusayā byantīhonti.
root_text.an5.57:8.1: ‘Na kho ahaññeveko byādhidhammo byādhiṁ anatīto, atha kho yāvatā sattānaṁ āgati gati cuti upapatti sabbe sattā byādhidhammā byādhiṁ anatītā’ti.
root_text.an5.57:8.2: Tassa taṁ ṭhānaṁ abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhato maggo sañjāyati.
root_text.an5.57:8.3: So taṁ maggaṁ āsevati bhāveti bahulīkaroti.
root_text.an5.57:8.4: Tassa taṁ maggaṁ āsevato bhāvayato bahulīkaroto saṁyojanāni sabbaso pahīyanti, anusayā byantīhonti.
root_text.an5.57:9.1: ‘Na kho ahaññeveko maraṇadhammo maraṇaṁ anatīto, atha kho yāvatā sattānaṁ āgati gati cuti upapatti sabbe sattā maraṇadhammā maraṇaṁ anatītā’ti.
root_text.an5.57:9.2: Tassa taṁ ṭhānaṁ abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhato maggo sañjāyati.
root_text.an5.57:9.3: So taṁ maggaṁ āsevati bhāveti bahulīkaroti.
root_text.an5.57:9.4: Tassa taṁ maggaṁ āsevato bhāvayato bahulīkaroto saṁyojanāni sabbaso pahīyanti, anusayā byantīhonti.
root_text.an5.57:10.1: ‘Na kho mayhevekassa sabbehi piyehi manāpehi nānābhāvo vinābhāvo, atha kho yāvatā sattānaṁ āgati gati cuti upapatti sabbesaṁ sattānaṁ piyehi manāpehi nānābhāvo vinābhāvo’ti.
root_text.an5.57:10.2: Tassa taṁ ṭhānaṁ abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhato maggo sañjāyati.
root_text.an5.57:10.3: So taṁ maggaṁ āsevati bhāveti bahulīkaroti.
root_text.an5.57:10.4: Tassa taṁ maggaṁ āsevato bhāvayato bahulīkaroto saṁyojanāni sabbaso pahīyanti, anusayā byantīhonti.
root_text.an5.57:11.1: ‘Na kho ahaññeveko kammassako kammadāyādo kammayoni kammabandhu kammapaṭisaraṇo, yaṁ kammaṁ karissāmi—kalyāṇaṁ vā pāpakaṁ vā—tassa dāyādo bhavissāmi;
root_text.an5.57:11.2: atha kho yāvatā sattānaṁ āgati gati cuti upapatti sabbe sattā kammassakā kammadāyādā kammayoni kammabandhu kammapaṭisaraṇā, yaṁ kammaṁ karissanti—kalyāṇaṁ vā pāpakaṁ vā—tassa dāyādā bhavissantī’ti.
root_text.an5.57:11.3: Tassa taṁ ṭhānaṁ abhiṇhaṁ paccavekkhato maggo sañjāyati.
root_text.an5.57:11.4: So taṁ maggaṁ āsevati bhāveti bahulīkaroti.
root_text.an5.57:11.5: Tassa taṁ maggaṁ āsevato bhāvayato bahulīkaroto saṁyojanāni sabbaso pahīyanti, anusayā byantīhontīti.

translation_text.an5.57:1.1: “Mendicants, a woman or a man, a layperson or a renunciate should often review these five subjects.
translation_text.an5.57:1.3: ‘I am liable to grow old, I am not exempt from old age.’ A woman or a man, a layperson or a renunciate should often review this.
translation_text.an5.57:1.4: ‘I am liable to get sick, I am not exempt from sickness.’ …
translation_text.an5.57:1.5: ‘I am liable to die, I am not exempt from death.’ …
translation_text.an5.57:1.6: ‘I must be parted and separated from all I hold dear and beloved.’ …
translation_text.an5.57:1.7: ‘I am the owner of my deeds and heir to my deeds. Deeds are my womb, my relative, and my refuge.
translation_text.an5.57:1.8: I shall be the heir of whatever deeds I do, whether good or bad.’
translation_text.an5.57:1.9: A woman or a man, a layperson or a renunciate should often review this.
translation_text.an5.57:2.1: What is the advantage for a woman or a man, a layperson or a renunciate of often reviewing this: ‘I am liable to grow old, I am not exempt from old age’?
translation_text.an5.57:2.2: There are sentient beings who, intoxicated with the vanity of youth, do bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an5.57:2.3: Reviewing this subject often, they entirely give up the vanity of youth, or at least reduce it.
translation_text.an5.57:2.4: This is the advantage for a woman or a man, a layperson or a renunciate of often reviewing this: ‘I am liable to grow old, I am not exempt from old age’.
translation_text.an5.57:3.1: What is the advantage of often reviewing this: ‘I am liable to get sick, I am not exempt from sickness’?
translation_text.an5.57:3.2: There are sentient beings who, drunk on the vanity of health, do bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an5.57:3.3: Reviewing this subject often, they entirely give up the vanity of health, or at least reduce it.
translation_text.an5.57:3.4: This is the advantage of often reviewing this: ‘I am liable to get sick, I am not exempt from sickness’.
translation_text.an5.57:4.1: What is the advantage of often reviewing this: ‘I am liable to die, I am not exempt from death’?
translation_text.an5.57:4.2: There are sentient beings who, drunk on the vanity of life, do bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an5.57:4.3: Reviewing this subject often, they entirely give up the vanity of life, or at least reduce it.
translation_text.an5.57:4.4: This is the advantage of often reviewing this: ‘I am liable to die, I am not exempt from death’.
translation_text.an5.57:5.1: What is the advantage of often reviewing this: ‘I must be parted and separated from all I hold dear and beloved’?
translation_text.an5.57:5.2: There are sentient beings who, aroused by desire and lust for their dear and beloved, do bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an5.57:5.3: Reviewing this subject often, they entirely give up desire and lust for their dear and beloved, or at least reduce it.
translation_text.an5.57:5.4: This is the advantage of often reviewing this: ‘I must be parted and separated from all I hold dear and beloved’.
translation_text.an5.57:6.1: What is the advantage of often reviewing like this: ‘I am the owner of my deeds and heir to my deeds. Deeds are my womb, my relative, and my refuge.
translation_text.an5.57:6.2: I shall be the heir of whatever deeds I do, whether good or bad’?
translation_text.an5.57:6.3: There are sentient beings who do bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an5.57:6.4: Reviewing this subject often, they entirely give up bad conduct, or at least reduce it.
translation_text.an5.57:6.5: This is the advantage for a woman or a man, a layperson or a renunciate of often reviewing like this: ‘I am the owner of my deeds and heir to my deeds. Deeds are my womb, my relative, and my refuge.
translation_text.an5.57:6.6: I shall be the heir of whatever deeds I do, whether good or bad.’
translation_text.an5.57:7.2: ‘It’s not just me who is liable to grow old, not being exempt from old age. For all sentient beings grow old according to their nature, as long as they come and go, pass away and are reborn.’
translation_text.an5.57:7.3: When they review this subject often, the path is born in them.
translation_text.an5.57:7.4: They cultivate, develop, and make much of it.
translation_text.an5.57:7.5: By doing so, they give up the fetters and eliminate the underlying tendencies.
translation_text.an5.57:8.1: ‘It’s not just me who is liable to get sick, not being exempt from sickness. For all sentient beings get sick according to their nature, as long as they come and go, pass away and are reborn.’
translation_text.an5.57:8.2: When they review this subject often, the path is born in them.
translation_text.an5.57:8.3: They cultivate, develop, and make much of it.
translation_text.an5.57:8.4: By doing so, they give up the fetters and eliminate the underlying tendencies.
translation_text.an5.57:9.1: ‘It’s not just me who is liable to die, not being exempt from death. For all sentient beings die according to their nature, as long as they come and go, pass away and are reborn.’
translation_text.an5.57:9.2: When they review this subject often, the path is born in them.
translation_text.an5.57:9.3: They cultivate, develop, and make much of it.
translation_text.an5.57:9.4: By doing so, they give up the fetters and eliminate the underlying tendencies.
translation_text.an5.57:10.1: ‘It’s not just me who must be parted and separated from all I hold dear and beloved. For all sentient beings must be parted and separated from all they hold dear and beloved, as long as they come and go, pass away and are reborn.’
translation_text.an5.57:10.2: When they review this subject often, the path is born in them.
translation_text.an5.57:10.3: They cultivate, develop, and make much of it.
translation_text.an5.57:10.4: By doing so, they give up the fetters and eliminate the underlying tendencies.
translation_text.an5.57:11.1: ‘It’s not just me who shall be the owner of my deeds and heir to my deeds.
translation_text.an5.57:11.2: For all sentient beings shall be the owners of their deeds and heirs to their deeds, as long as they come and go, pass away and are reborn.’
translation_text.an5.57:11.3: When they review this subject often, the path is born in them.
translation_text.an5.57:11.4: They cultivate, develop, and make much of it.
translation_text.an5.57:11.5: By doing so, they give up the fetters and eliminate the underlying tendencies.
translation_text.an5.57:12.2: and so are old age and death.
translation_text.an5.57:12.3: Though this is how their nature is,
translation_text.an5.57:13.1: If I were to be disgusted
translation_text.an5.57:13.2: with creatures whose nature is such,
translation_text.an5.57:13.3: it would not be appropriate for me,
translation_text.an5.57:13.4: since my life is just the same.
translation_text.an5.57:14.2: I understood the truth without attachments.
translation_text.an5.57:16.4: I’m committed to the spiritual life.”
